items.title: Fiji Immigration External Management System
items.tagline: A secure citizen-facing portal for online immigration services.

blocks.heading: The Problem
paragraphs: Individuals, employers and businesses in Fiji needed to apply for visas, work permits, residence permits, citizenship, investor permits and study permits — but the process depended on in-person visits and paper forms. This created long processing times, limited transparency for applicants, and a heavy administrative burden.
The goal was a secure, self-service digital platform that let applicants submit and track immigration applications entirely online, across a range of distinct service types and applicant roles.

blocks.heading: The Solution
paragraphs: I built the citizen-facing frontend as a modern, role-based Angular application. It streamlines the full journey — application submission, document uploads, appointment scheduling, payment integration, application tracking and status notifications — behind a single, consistent experience.

Distinct immigration services were modelled as reusable, configurable application flows, so each permit type shares the same underlying form, validation and submission infrastructure while presenting the fields relevant to that service.

blocks.heading: Architecture & Approach
paragraphs: The app uses standalone Angular components with Signals for reactive state and RxJS for asynchronous data streams, keeping change detection efficient across large multi-step forms. Tailwind CSS drives a responsive, accessible design system, and all data flows through typed REST API integrations with robust loading and error handling.

highlights: Online application for visas, work, residence, citizenship, investor and study permits
Document uploads, appointment scheduling and integrated payments
Real-time application tracking and status notifications
Role-based experience for individuals, employers and businesses

items.title: Fiji Immigration Internal Management System
items.tagline: An enterprise operations platform for immigration officers and administrators.

blocks.heading: The Problem
paragraphs: Immigration officers and government administrators needed a single internal system to manage end-to-end immigration operations — assessing applications, verifying documents, approving permits and monitoring compliance — across many services that were previously handled through disconnected manual processes.
The platform had to enforce strict role-based access control while remaining fast and usable for officers processing high volumes of applications daily.

blocks.heading: The Solution
paragraphs: I engineered the internal frontend as an enterprise-grade Angular platform supporting application assessment, document verification, workflow automation, permit approvals, visa and citizenship processing, border operations, compliance monitoring, reporting and user administration.
Complex operational workflows were decomposed into modular, reusable components so that new immigration services and approval steps could be added without reworking the core.

blocks.heading: Architecture & Approach
paragraphs: The system pairs a signal-driven state layer with RxJS-based data orchestration to keep dense, data-heavy screens responsive. Role-based access control gates features and routes per user type, and a shared component and design system built with Tailwind CSS keeps the large surface area visually and behaviourally consistent.

highlights: Application assessment, document verification and workflow automation
Permit approvals, visa processing and citizenship management
Border operations, compliance monitoring and reporting
Granular role-based access control and user administration

items.title: PRIMS Member Portal
items.tagline: A self-service pension portal that puts members in control of their retirement accounts.

blocks.heading: The Problem
paragraphs: Pension members had limited visibility into their retirement accounts and had to rely on manual, offline channels for routine tasks such as checking balances, requesting statements and submitting claims.
They needed a secure, always-available way to manage their retirement information and self-serve common requests.

blocks.heading: The Solution
paragraphs: I built a secure self-service portal giving members real-time access to contribution history, account balances and benefit statements, along with profile management, document services, claim submissions and beneficiary management.
The interface was designed to make complex pension data approachable and to guide members through claims and updates with clear, responsive flows.

blocks.heading: Architecture & Approach
paragraphs: The portal is built on Angular with Signals and RxJS for reactive data handling, and Angular Material for an accessible, consistent component foundation. Member data is served through typed REST integrations with careful handling of loading, empty and error states.

highlights: Real-time contribution history, balances and benefit statements
Online claim submission and document services
Profile and beneficiary management
Responsive, accessible member experience

items.title: VNPF blo mi — Member Mobile Application
items.tagline: A cross-platform provident-fund app putting member services in every member's pocket.

blocks.heading: The Problem
paragraphs: Members of the Vanuatu National Provident Fund (VNPF) needed convenient, anytime-anywhere access to their fund services — but existing channels required visiting offices or using desktop systems that weren't practical for many members.
The solution had to run natively on both iOS and Android while remaining maintainable from a single codebase.

blocks.heading: The Solution
paragraphs: I developed a secure cross-platform mobile application giving members real-time access to account balances, contribution history, loan details, withdrawal requests, insurance information and profile management. Members can submit loan, insurance and withdrawal applications directly from the app.
The experience was designed to feel native and responsive on a wide range of devices, with an emphasis on clarity and trust given the financial nature of the data.

blocks.heading: Architecture & Approach
paragraphs: Built with Ionic and Angular and packaged natively via Capacitor, the app shares one Angular codebase across iOS and Android. RxJS powers the reactive data layer, and secure typed REST integrations connect the app to VNPF's member services.

highlights: Real-time balances, contribution history and loan details
In-app loan, insurance and withdrawal applications
Native iOS & Android from a single Ionic + Angular codebase
Secure, member-focused financial experience

items.title: Zellavora AI Resume Builder
items.tagline: Guided inputs to tailored, ATS-friendly resumes — with a live editor and AI content.

blocks.heading: The Problem
paragraphs: Writing a strong, ATS-friendly resume is hard: people struggle to phrase their experience well and to format it so applicant tracking systems can parse it. Generic templates don't help with the actual content.
The aim was a product that turns simple guided inputs into polished, tailored resume content without forcing users to fight with formatting.

blocks.heading: The Solution
paragraphs: I built an AI-powered resume builder that pairs a live preview editor with AI content generation and one-click PDF export. Users answer guided prompts and the app generates tailored, ATS-friendly resume sections they can refine in real time.
The live editor reflects every change instantly, so users always see exactly what their exported resume will look like.

blocks.heading: Architecture & Approach
paragraphs: The app is built with Angular and RxJS for a reactive live-preview editor, Tailwind CSS for the design system, and an AI integration layer that generates and refines resume content. Export produces a clean, ATS-parseable PDF in a single click.

highlights: AI-generated, ATS-friendly resume content from guided inputs
Live preview editor with real-time updates
One-click PDF export
Tailored output per role and experience
